minced-meat-sauce: A hearty woodland ragout of boar, neck meat, and carrot.
Brown chopped boar and poultry or lizard meat; add diced carrot, water, salt, and herbs, then simmer until thick.
queens-jam: A bright preserve combining two kinds of forest berries.
Simmer raspberries and blueberries with a little sugar and lemon until glossy; cool before serving.
carrot-soup: A simple mushroom-and-carrot soup built for long journeys.
Soften mushrooms and carrots in butter, add stock, simmer until tender, then blend and season.
deer-stew: A rich venison stew balanced with carrot and blueberries.
Brown venison, then gently braise it with carrot, stock, herbs, and a spoonful of crushed blueberries.
muckshake: A strange sweet-tart berry drink inspired by the Swamp.
Blend raspberries and blueberries with yogurt, lime, honey, and a pinch of spirulina for its eerie color.
turnip-stew: A savory turnip and pork stew from the Swamp tier.
Brown pork, add cubed turnips and stock, then simmer with thyme until the vegetables are soft.
sausages: Rustic pork sausages fragranced with woodland herbs.
Mix ground pork with salt, pepper, garlic, and dried herbs; shape into links or patties and pan-cook thoroughly.
black-soup: A dark, earthy turnip soup with a bittersweet finish.
Roast turnip and beet, simmer with stock and onion, blend smooth, then balance with a little honey.
serpent-stew: A prized ocean stew pairing rich fish with mushroom and honey.
Sear firm white fish, then simmer briefly with mushrooms, seafood stock, herbs, and a touch of honey.
wolf-skewer: A mountain skewer of game meat, mushroom, and onion.
Thread venison or lamb, mushrooms, and onion onto skewers; season and grill until browned and cooked through.
eyescream: An icy blue fantasy dessert with a sharp, frosty look.
Churn vanilla ice cream with blueberry puree and lime zest; add candy eyes just before serving.
fish-wraps: Warm flatbread wrapped around flaky cooked fish.
Fill soft flatbreads with seasoned grilled fish, cabbage, herbs, and a spoonful of yogurt sauce.
lox-meat-pie: A substantial Plains pie filled with game and tart berries.
Encase browned beef or venison and crushed berries in pastry; vent and bake until deeply golden.
blood-pudding: A dark savory pudding inspired by Nordic blood sausage.
For an approachable version, combine barley, onion, beet puree, herbs, and sausage meat; bake until firm.
meat-platter: A Mistlands feast bringing together several roasted meats.
Roast small portions of beef, rabbit, and poultry with mushrooms; slice and arrange with pan juices.
honey-glazed-chicken: Roasted chicken lacquered with honey and mushrooms.
Brush chicken with honey, mustard, and salt; roast over mushrooms until caramelized and fully cooked.
misthare-supreme: A refined rabbit roast with mushroom and carrot.
Brown rabbit or chicken thighs, then roast with carrots, mushrooms, stock, and thyme until tender.
mushroom-omelette: A soft omelette generously filled with sautéed mushrooms.
Brown sliced mushrooms in butter, add beaten eggs, cook gently, fold, and finish with black pepper.
stuffed-mushroom: Large mushroom caps baked with a savory vegetable filling.
Fill mushroom caps with breadcrumbs, minced turnip, garlic, and herbs; bake until browned and juicy.
yggdrasil-porridge: A luminous fantasy porridge inspired by the world tree.
Simmer oats in milk until creamy; sweeten with maple syrup and top with blueberries and toasted seeds.
appalachili: A fiery Appalachian chili made from wasteland staples.
Brown ground beef with onion; add beans, tomato, chili powder, and hot sauce, then simmer until thick.
baked-bloatfly: A roasted insect dish reimagined as a crisp stuffed mushroom.
Stuff portobello caps with seasoned breadcrumbs and cheese; bake until crisp at the edges.
blackberry-honey-crisp: A warm berry crisp sweetened with honey.
Toss blackberries with honey and lemon; cover with an oat-butter crumble and bake until bubbling.
brain-bombs: A playful iced pastry with an unmistakably post-apocalyptic name.
Bake small spiced cakes, glaze with pink icing, and pipe looping lines on top for a brain-like finish.
cranberry-meatball-grinder: A toasted meatball sandwich sharpened by cranberry sauce.
Fill a roll with cooked meatballs, tomato sauce, and cranberry relish; top with cheese and broil.
deathclaw-wellington: A wasteland showpiece of meat and mushrooms wrapped in pastry.
Coat a seared beef fillet with mushroom duxelles, wrap in puff pastry, and bake until golden.
fasnacht-donut: A festive fried pastry dusted or glazed with sugar.
Cut rings from enriched yeast dough, fry until golden, then coat with cinnamon sugar or glaze.
fried-deerskins: A crunchy fried snack translated into seasoned venison strips.
Coat thin venison or beef strips in seasoned flour and shallow-fry until crisp and cooked through.
glowing-fungus-puree: A vivid, silky mushroom puree with an eerie green tint.
Sauté mushrooms and spinach with garlic, blend with stock and cream, then season with lemon.
iguana-soup: A clear, warming soup built around tender meat and vegetables.
Simmer chicken with onion, carrot, celery, herbs, and stock; shred the meat and return it to the pot.
mirelurk-cake-bloodleaf-aioli: A crab-style cake served with a crimson herb aioli.
Pan-fry crab cakes made with breadcrumbs and egg; serve with beet-tinted garlic mayonnaise.
pepperoni-roll: A West Virginia bread roll baked around pepperoni.
Wrap bread dough around pepperoni sticks and a little cheese; proof, then bake until browned.
pumpkin-pie: A familiar spiced custard pie made with pumpkin.
Whisk pumpkin puree with eggs, milk, sugar, and warm spices; pour into pastry and bake until set.
radscorpion-kebab: A smoky skewer translated into spicy shrimp and vegetables.
Skewer shrimp, peppers, and onion; brush with chili oil and grill until the shrimp are opaque.
ribeye-steak: A simply seared ribeye with a deeply browned crust.
Salt the steak, sear in a very hot pan, baste with butter and garlic, then rest before slicing.
silt-bean-puree: A smooth bean puree seasoned with aromatic vegetables.
Simmer white beans with onion and garlic, then blend with olive oil, lemon, and enough stock to loosen.
smoked-mirelurk-fillets: Smoky seafood fillets with a sweet-savory glaze.
Brush firm fish with paprika, brown sugar, salt, and oil; smoke or oven-roast until flaky.
squirrel-on-a-stick: A campfire skewer of small-game meat and vegetables.
Thread chicken thigh pieces and vegetables onto skewers, season boldly, and grill until fully cooked.
tato-salad: A creamy wasteland-style potato and tomato salad.
Boil diced potatoes until tender, cool, then fold with tomato, celery, herbs, mustard, and mayonnaise.
tasty-squirrel-stew: A thick camp stew of meat, vegetables, and herbs.
Brown chicken thighs, add potatoes, carrots, onion, herbs, and stock, then simmer until tender.
plain-big-game: A substantial campfire portion of plain big-game meat.
Salt a thick venison or beef steak and grill over high heat until browned and safely cooked.
minty-big-game: Big-game meat seasoned with wild mint for a fresh finish.
Rub venison or beef with chopped mint, salt, and oil; grill, rest, and finish with more mint.
oregano-big-game: Big-game meat grilled with pungent oregano.
Rub venison or beef with oregano, garlic, salt, and oil, then grill and rest before slicing.
thyme-big-game: Big-game meat perfumed with woodland thyme.
Season venison or beef with thyme, pepper, salt, and oil; grill until done and rest well.
plain-prime-beef: A high-quality cut of beef cooked simply over the fire.
Salt a beef steak generously, grill to your preferred doneness, and rest before serving.
minty-prime-beef: Prime beef brightened with chopped wild mint.
Marinate beef briefly with mint, oil, and salt; sear or grill, then rest before slicing.
oregano-prime-beef: Prime beef with a robust oregano crust.
Press oregano, garlic, salt, and pepper onto beef; sear hard, finish gently, and rest.
thyme-prime-beef: Prime beef basted with thyme at the campfire.
Sear beef, then baste with butter, garlic, and thyme until done; rest before serving.
plain-mature-venison: Lean mature venison roasted without seasoning herbs.
Salt venison, sear it quickly, then finish over moderate heat and rest to keep it tender.
minty-mature-venison: Mature venison paired with cooling wild mint.
Grill salted venison and spoon over chopped mint mixed with vinegar, oil, and a pinch of sugar.
oregano-mature-venison: Mature venison seasoned with oregano and garlic.
Rub venison with oregano, garlic, oil, and salt; sear quickly and rest before carving.
thyme-mature-venison: Mature venison with fragrant thyme and pepper.
Season venison with thyme, salt, and pepper; pan-sear in butter and rest before slicing.
plain-tender-pork: A juicy campfire cut of tender pork.
Salt a pork chop and grill until browned and its center reaches a safe temperature; rest briefly.
minty-tender-pork: Tender pork given a fresh wild-mint seasoning.
Rub pork with mint, lemon zest, salt, and oil; grill until safely cooked and rest.
oregano-tender-pork: Tender pork with a savory oregano crust.
Coat pork with oregano, garlic, salt, and oil; sear, then cook through over gentler heat.
thyme-tender-pork: Tender pork fragranced with thyme at the fire.
Season pork with thyme, mustard, salt, and pepper; grill until safely cooked and rest.
plain-succulent-fish: A moist fillet of succulent fish cooked over coals.
Oil and salt a salmon or trout fillet, then grill skin-side down until just flaky.
minty-succulent-fish: Succulent fish lifted with fresh wild mint.
Grill a seasoned fish fillet and finish with mint, lemon juice, and olive oil.
oregano-succulent-fish: Succulent fish with oregano and a smoky campfire edge.
Rub fish with oregano, paprika, salt, and oil; grill until opaque and flaky.
thyme-succulent-fish: Succulent fish delicately seasoned with thyme.
Top fish with thyme, lemon slices, salt, and butter; wrap loosely in foil and cook over coals.
